title: [H2] Add fallback authorization policy requiring authentication
description:
## Severity: HIGH

## Summary
There is no fallback authorization policy. Any controller endpoint without an explicit [Authorize] or [HasPermission] attribute is accessible anonymously by default. This is a defense-in-depth gap.

## Finding
**File:** src/Modules/Identity/Foundry.Identity.Infrastructure/Authorization/PermissionAuthorizationPolicyProvider.cs:33-34

GetFallbackPolicyAsync() delegates to the default provider, which returns null (no fallback). ASP.NET Core best practice is to require authenticated users globally via fallback policy, then opt out with [AllowAnonymous] where needed.

Currently all controllers do have explicit [Authorize] or [AllowAnonymous], so the immediate risk is low. But any future endpoint added without an attribute would be silently open.

## Required Fix
Configure the fallback policy to require authenticated users. In the service registration (likely in AddIdentityModule or AddApiServices):

```csharp
services.AddAuthorization(options =>
{
    options.FallbackPolicy = new AuthorizationPolicyBuilder()
        .RequireAuthenticatedUser()
        .Build();
});
```

After this change, verify that all intentionally-anonymous endpoints have [AllowAnonymous]:
- AuthController (login/refresh) — already has it
- ScimController — already has it (uses own auth middleware)
- ChangelogController — already has it
- Health check endpoints — mapped via MapHealthChecks (may need .AllowAnonymous())
- Root info endpoint at / — may need .AllowAnonymous()
- OpenAPI/Scalar endpoints — check if they need anonymous access in dev

title: [L8] Sanitize user-supplied filenames in Storage module
description:
## Severity: LOW

## Finding
**File:** src/Modules/Storage/Foundry.Storage.Application/Commands/UploadFile/UploadFileHandler.cs:40,55

The original user-supplied file.FileName is stored in StoredFile.FileName (metadata) without sanitization. The actual storage key uses a server-generated GUID, so the filename is only metadata.

However, if the filename is later returned to clients and used in a Content-Disposition header without sanitization, it could cause header injection or UI confusion.

## Fix
Sanitize the stored filename: strip path components, replace or remove special characters, limit length. The actual storage uses GUID keys so this is purely for display/download metadata.

P0 #5 - Multiple integration events in Shared.Contracts are missing TenantId, preventing consumers from routing/filtering by tenant.

CURRENT STATE - Events MISSING TenantId:
Billing (4 events):
- InvoiceCreatedEvent (InvoiceId, UserId, UserEmail, InvoiceNumber, Amount, Currency, DueDate)
- InvoicePaidEvent (InvoiceId, PaymentId, UserId, InvoiceNumber, Amount, Currency, PaidAt)
- InvoiceOverdueEvent (InvoiceId, UserId, UserEmail, InvoiceNumber, Amount, Currency, DueDate)
- PaymentReceivedEvent (PaymentId, InvoiceId, UserId, UserEmail, Amount, Currency, PaymentMethod, PaidAt)

Identity (2+ events):
- UserRegisteredEvent (UserId, Email, FirstName, LastName)
- OrganizationCreatedEvent (OrganizationId, Name, Domain?)
- Plus: OrganizationMemberAddedEvent, OrganizationMemberRemovedEvent, UserRoleChangedEvent, PasswordResetRequestedEvent (need verification)

Notifications (1 event):
- NotificationCreatedEvent (NotificationId, UserId, Title, Type, CreatedAt)

Email (1 event):

- Add 'required Guid TenantId { get; init; }' to all events listed above

title: [M2] Fix JSON string interpolation in InvoiceRepositoryExtensions
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
InvoiceRepositoryExtensions uses string interpolation to build JSON for EF.Functions.JsonContains, which can break with special characters in input.

## Finding
**File:** src/Modules/Billing/Foundry.Billing.Infrastructure/Persistence/Repositories/InvoiceRepositoryExtensions.cs:18,50

```csharp
string jsonFilter = $"{{\"{fieldKey}\": \"{fieldValue}\"}}";
```

If fieldKey or fieldValue contain double quotes or backslashes, the JSON structure breaks. A crafted fieldValue like: value"}, "admin": "true could inject additional JSON keys.

Note: Line 33 in FindByCustomFieldsAsync correctly uses JsonSerializer.Serialize(criteria) which handles escaping. The single-field methods at lines 18 and 50 do not.

## Impact
Potential JSON injection if user-controlled values reach these methods. Could cause unexpected query behavior.

## Required Fix
Replace the string interpolation at lines 18 and 50 with JsonSerializer.Serialize:
```csharp
string jsonFilter = JsonSerializer.Serialize(new Dictionary<string, string> { { fieldKey, fieldValue } });
```
This matches the pattern already used at line 33.

## Summary

## Finding
**File:** src/Foundry.Api/Program.cs:326-334

```csharp
app.MapGet("/", () => Results.Ok(new
{
    Name = "Foundry API",
    Version = "1.0.0",
    Environment = app.Environment.EnvironmentName,
    Documentation = "/scalar/v1",
    Health = "/health",
    BackgroundJobs = "/hangfire"
})).ExcludeFromDescription();
```

## Impact
Attackers learn the deployment environment (Production/Staging/Development) and discover administrative endpoints.

## Required Fix
Option A: Make this endpoint development-only
Option B: Remove Environment name and internal paths (BackgroundJobs, Documentation) from production responses. Keep only Name, Version, and Health.

title: [H5] Validate SignalR hub group membership against tenant
description:
## Severity: HIGH

## Summary
The SignalR RealtimeHub JoinGroup() method accepts any arbitrary group ID without authorization checks. Any authenticated user can join any group and receive real-time messages for other tenants.

## Finding
**File:** src/Foundry.Api/Hubs/RealtimeHub.cs:50-53

JoinGroup(string groupId) adds the caller to the requested SignalR group without validating:
- Whether the group belongs to the caller's tenant
- Whether the caller has access to the resource the group represents

The hub does require authentication ([Authorize] at class level), but any authenticated user from Tenant A could join groups like 'project:tenant-b-project-id' and receive all real-time updates intended for Tenant B.

## Impact
Cross-tenant data leakage via SignalR real-time messages. An attacker could monitor another tenant's notifications, activity feeds, and other real-time events.

## Required Fix
1. Validate that the authenticated user has access to the requested group before calling Groups.AddToGroupAsync()
2. Group IDs should follow a consistent format that embeds tenant context (e.g., 'tenant:{tenantId}:resource:{resourceId}')
3. Extract the tenant from the group ID and compare against the caller's ITenantContext
4. Consider using a group naming convention that makes tenant validation straightforward
5. Add tests to verify cross-tenant group join is rejected with appropriate error

title: [M1] Add centralized tenant filtering for Dapper queries
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
Dapper queries bypass EF Core's HasQueryFilter tenant isolation. All Dapper services accept tenantId as a method parameter and manually include WHERE TenantId = @TenantId, but this relies on callers passing the correct tenant ID rather than being automatically enforced.

## Findings
**Files:**
- src/Modules/Billing/Foundry.Billing.Infrastructure/Services/RevenueReportService.cs
- src/Modules/Billing/Foundry.Billing.Infrastructure/Services/InvoiceQueryService.cs
- src/Modules/Billing/Foundry.Billing.Infrastructure/Services/InvoiceReportService.cs
- src/Modules/Billing/Foundry.Billing.Infrastructure/Services/PaymentReportService.cs

All four services take Guid tenantId as a method parameter rather than reading from ITenantContext. The calling code must pass the correct tenant ID.

Note: All queries ARE properly parameterized with @TenantId — this is not an injection issue. It's a design concern about tenant isolation enforcement.

## Impact
If a caller passes the wrong tenantId (or a future developer creates a query without the filter), cross-tenant data access is possible. No centralized enforcement layer exists for Dapper queries.

## Required Fix
Consider one or more of:
1. Create a TenantAwareDapperService wrapper that injects ITenantContext and automatically adds tenant filtering
2. Change Dapper services to inject ITenantContext directly and use its TenantId, removing the parameter
3. Add an architecture test that verifies all Dapper queries include tenant filtering
4. At minimum, add unit tests that verify tenant filtering is present in all Dapper queries

title: [C1] Fix Elsa Workflow API hardcoded signing key and unprotected endpoints
description:
## Severity: CRITICAL

## Summary
The Elsa workflow engine uses its own JWT identity system with a hardcoded fallback signing key and its API endpoints are not behind the application's Keycloak authentication.

## Findings

### Hardcoded Signing Key
**File:** src/Shared/Foundry.Shared.Infrastructure/Workflows/ElsaExtensions.cs:42-43

The Elsa identity signing key falls back to a hardcoded string:
```csharp
identity.TokenOptions = options =>
    options.SigningKey = configuration["Elsa:Identity:SigningKey"]
        ?? "foundry-default-elsa-signing-key-replace-in-production";
```

No appsettings files (Development, Staging, Production) override this value, meaning ALL environments use this hardcoded key.

### Unprotected API Endpoints
**File:** src/Foundry.Api/Program.cs:370-374

`app.UseWorkflowsApi()` and `app.UseWorkflows()` are called after `app.UseAuthorization()` (line 356), but the Elsa API endpoints use Elsa's own identity system (not Keycloak JWT auth). Elsa's `UseAdminUserProvider()` creates a default admin user. The Elsa API is publicly accessible at whatever route prefix is configured.

## Impact
Anyone who knows the default signing key can forge Elsa admin tokens and access the workflow management API to create, modify, or delete workflows — effectively arbitrary code execution on the server.

## Required Fix
1. Remove the hardcoded fallback. Throw at startup if `Elsa:Identity:SigningKey` is not configured in non-Development environments.
2. Either restrict the Elsa API to development environments only, or put it behind the application's `[Authorize]` with an admin-only policy.
3. Add `Elsa:Identity:SigningKey` to production/staging env var overrides (appsettings.Production.json, appsettings.Staging.json).

title: [H3] Add permission enforcement to Configuration module controllers
description:
## Severity: HIGH

## Summary
FeatureFlagsController and CustomFieldsController use [Authorize] but have no [HasPermission] attributes. Any authenticated user (including basic 'user' role) can create/update/delete feature flags and custom fields, despite comments saying 'admin only'.

## Findings
**Files:**
- src/Modules/Configuration/Foundry.Configuration.Api/Controllers/FeatureFlagsController.cs:27 — Only [Authorize], comments say 'admin only' but no enforcement
- src/Modules/Configuration/Foundry.Configuration.Api/Controllers/CustomFieldsController.cs:16 — Only [Authorize]

Both controllers have comments on individual actions like '(admin only)' but this is never enforced via [HasPermission].

## Impact
Any authenticated user can:
- Create, update, and delete feature flags
- Create, update, and delete custom field definitions
- Create/delete feature flag overrides for any tenant

## Required Fix
1. Define appropriate permission types (e.g., PermissionType.ConfigurationManage or more granular read/write split)
2. Add [HasPermission(PermissionType.ConfigurationManage)] to both controllers at the class level (or per-action for granular control)
3. Add the new permission(s) to RolePermissionMapping.cs — map to 'admin' and possibly 'manager' roles
4. Add corresponding tests to verify unauthorized users get 403

title: Add foundry.module span attribute via middleware
description:
Add a foundry.module attribute to every span so you can filter/group traces by module in Grafana.

Two middleware layers needed:

1. ASP.NET Core middleware — for HTTP request spans:
   - Extract module from route or controller namespace (e.g., /api/billing/invoices → Billing)
   - Set Activity.Current?.SetTag("foundry.module", moduleName)
   - Create src/Foundry.Api/Middleware/ModuleActivityTagMiddleware.cs
   - Register in Program.cs pipeline

2. Wolverine middleware — for message handler spans:
   - Derive module from message type namespace (Foundry.{Module}.* → Module)
   - Set Activity.Current?.SetTag("foundry.module", moduleName)
   - Set LogContext.PushProperty("Module", moduleName) for Serilog consistency
   - Create in Shared.Kernel or Shared.Infrastructure as a Wolverine middleware

Also consider adding tenant.id as a span tag when ITenantContext is available.

title: Standardize naming conventions across modules
description:
Fix inconsistent naming patterns found across modules:

1. Domain event suffix: 3 patterns exist — *DomainEvent (Billing), *Event (Catalog), bare past-tense (event-sourced). Standardize: *DomainEvent for EF Core modules, bare past-tense for Marten event-sourced modules.
2. Integration events: Sales uses bare names (OrderPlaced, OrderCancelled, PaymentCompleted) while every other module uses *Event suffix. Add Event suffix and move to Events/ subfolder.
3. CancellationToken: split between cancellationToken (Billing, Notifications) and ct (Catalog, Identity, Inventory). Pick ct (shorter, modern convention).
4. Repository methods: Remove (Billing) vs Delete (Catalog). Pick one verb consistently.
5. Query naming: GetInvoiceById vs GetItem. Pick bare names (GetInvoice).
6. Duplicate PagedResult<T> in Catalog and Notifications with different property names. Extract to Shared.Kernel.
7. SendEmailRequestedEvent lives in Commands/ folder but named *Event. Rename or move.
notes: 2026-02-23 verification: 3 of 7 claims confirmed. Still valid: (1) domain event suffix split (*DomainEvent vs *Event), (2) CancellationToken naming (cancellationToken vs ct), (3) Remove vs Delete in repos (actually 3+ conventions). NOT valid: Sales integration events (none exist), duplicate PagedResult (names match), SendEmailRequestedEvent (correctly in Events/).

title: Fix API & messaging integration test failures
description: 18 integration tests fail across 2 projects due to infrastructure connection errors. Api.Tests (9 failures) connects directly to localhost:5432 Postgres. Messaging.IntegrationTests (9 failures) needs RabbitMQ. These may need Testcontainer support or require running Docker infrastructure.
close_reason: Both child issues fixed. Root cause: FoundryApiFactory set Testcontainer connection strings via ConfigureAppConfiguration which ran too late — modules captured default connection strings at service registration time. Fix: set environment variables in InitializeAsync() before WebApplicationFactory builds. All Api.Tests (23/23) and Messaging.IntegrationTests (9 pass, 3 valid skips) now pass.

title: Fix Api.Tests integration failures (9 failures)
description: 9 tests fail with Npgsql connection refused to 127.0.0.1:5432. Includes HealthCheckTests (Root, Health, HealthLive, HealthReady) and RealtimeHubIntegrationTests (5 tests). These connect directly to localhost Postgres rather than using Testcontainers — may need Testcontainer support added.
close_reason: Fixed by setting environment variables in FoundryApiFactory.InitializeAsync() for connection strings. Root cause: modules capture connection strings at service registration time (before WebApplicationFactory.ConfigureAppConfiguration applies), so the in-memory config override was too late. Environment variables are read by EnvironmentVariablesConfigurationProvider during WebApplication.CreateBuilder(), making them available when modules register.

title: Fix Messaging.IntegrationTests (9 failures)

close_reason: All 9 test failures fixed. Root cause: FoundryApiFactory's ConfigureAppConfiguration ran too late — modules eagerly captured connection strings before Testcontainer overrides took effect. Fix: set environment variables in InitializeAsync() so they're available when WebApplication.CreateBuilder() runs. 3 skipped tests have valid Skip attributes (Wolverine MultipleHandlerBehavior.Separated limitation).

title: Create per-module ModuleTelemetry classes with ActivitySource and Meter
description:
Create a static telemetry class in each module's Application layer with a module-scoped ActivitySource and Meter. This enables filtering traces and metrics by module in Grafana.

Pattern for each module:
```csharp
// In {Module}.Application/Telemetry/{Module}ModuleTelemetry.cs
public static class {Module}ModuleTelemetry
{
    public static readonly ActivitySource ActivitySource = new("Foundry.{Module}");
    public static readonly Meter Meter = new("Foundry.{Module}", "1.0");
}
```

Modules to create telemetry classes for:
- Identity (has metrics on shared Meter, needs own Meter)
- Billing (has metrics on shared Meter, needs own Meter)
- Sales (has metrics on shared Meter, needs own Meter)
- Scheduling (already has ActivitySource in SchedulingService, refactor to centralized class)
- Catalog (already has ActivitySource in CatalogReadService, refactor to centralized class)
- Inventory
- Email
- Notifications
- Storage
- Assets
- Configuration
- Onboarding

Also update Diagnostics.cs in Shared.Kernel to add a CreateMeter factory method alongside the existing CreateActivitySource.

Migrate existing metrics from Diagnostics.Meter to per-module Meters:
- Sales: TransactionProjection.cs — move counters to SalesModuleTelemetry.Meter
- Identity: KeycloakSsoService.cs — move counters to IdentityModuleTelemetry.Meter  
- Billing: InvoiceCreatedDomainEventHandler.cs — move counters to BillingModuleTelemetry.Meter

title: [M4] Restrict health check endpoint information disclosure
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
Health check endpoints (/health, /health/ready) are unauthenticated and return detailed information including individual service names, durations, descriptions, and exception messages.

## Finding
**File:** src/Foundry.Api/Program.cs:308-323, 408-424

The health check response writer returns: individual check names (postgresql, rabbitmq, redis, hangfire), their status, response durations in ms, descriptions, and exception messages. These endpoints are mapped without any authentication.

## Impact
Information disclosure. Attackers can discover infrastructure topology (what databases, message brokers, and caches are in use), identify slow or failing services, and read error messages that may contain connection details or internal paths.

## Required Fix
Option A (Recommended): In production, return only aggregate status for unauthenticated callers. Require authentication for the detailed /health endpoint. Keep /health/live public (no details, just 200/503).

Option B: Strip detailed information from production responses — return only overall status and individual check names/status without durations, descriptions, or error messages.

Note: /health/live already returns no details (Predicate = _ => false). This is correct for liveness probes.

title: [M9] Add virus/malware scanning for file uploads
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
No antivirus or malware scanning is performed on uploaded files. Already documented as a known issue in src/Modules/Storage/CLAUDE.md.

## Finding
The Storage module validates file uploads for:
- Magic byte validation (JPEG, PNG, PDF, GIF, ZIP) — prevents content-type spoofing
- File name length (500 chars), content type (200 chars), path (500 chars)
- File size (> 0, max 100MB at controller level)
- Bucket-level content type and size restrictions

However, no virus/malware scanning is performed on the actual file contents.

## Impact
Malicious files (malware, ransomware) could be uploaded and stored, potentially affecting users who download them.

## Required Fix
Consider integrating:
1. ClamAV (open-source, Docker-based) — most common for .NET apps
2. VirusTotal API — cloud-based scanning
3. Windows Defender on Windows servers

Implementation approach:
- Add an IFileScanner interface in Storage.Application
- Implement ClamAV scanner in Storage.Infrastructure  
- Call scanner in UploadFileHandler before persisting
- Return 422 if malware detected
- Add health check for ClamAV availability
- Consider async scanning for large files (scan in background, quarantine until clean)

title: [M3] Validate production CORS AllowedOrigins is not empty
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
Production and Staging appsettings set AllowedOrigins to an empty array. When WithOrigins() is called with an empty array, behavior is ambiguous — it may block all CORS requests (breaking the frontend) or enforce nothing.

## Findings
**Files:**
- src/Foundry.Api/appsettings.Production.json:46-48 — "AllowedOrigins": []
- src/Foundry.Api/appsettings.Staging.json:44-46 — "AllowedOrigins": []
- src/Foundry.Api/Extensions/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:80-89 — CORS registration

The expectation is that production origins are set via environment variables, but there's no validation that they're actually configured.

## Impact
Either the frontend won't work (broken CORS) or no origin restriction (open CORS). Both are problematic.

## Required Fix
1. Add startup validation that throws if AllowedOrigins is empty in non-Development environments
2. Ensure production deployment documentation mentions setting Cors:AllowedOrigins
3. Consider adding a comment in the appsettings files noting that these must be overridden via env vars

title: [C2] Fix SCIM token stored plaintext on initial creation
description:
## Severity: CRITICAL

## Summary
ScimConfiguration.Create() stores the SCIM bearer token in plaintext in the database. The RegenerateToken() method correctly SHA256 hashes it, but the first-ever token is stored raw.

## Finding
**File:** src/Modules/Identity/Foundry.Identity.Domain/Entities/ScimConfiguration.cs:41-53

```csharp
public static ScimConfiguration Create(TenantId tenantId, Guid createdByUserId)
{
    (string? token, string? prefix) = GenerateTokenAndPrefix();
    // ...
    return new ScimConfiguration(
        tenantId,
        token,      // <-- PLAINTEXT stored in BearerToken column
        prefix,
        expiresAt,
        createdByUserId);
}
```

Compare with RegenerateToken() (lines 56-67) which correctly calls HashToken() before storing.

## Impact
A database compromise would expose the initial SCIM token, granting full SCIM User/Group CRUD access to any tenant that never regenerated their token.

## Required Fix
Hash the token in Create() the same way RegenerateToken() does. The plaintext token needs to be returned to the caller (so they can use it) but the BearerToken field in the entity should store only the SHA256 hash. This likely means Create() should return a tuple of (ScimConfiguration entity, string plaintextToken) or similar pattern.

Also check: does the handler that calls Create() return the plaintext token to the API caller? It needs to, since this is the only time the user sees it. Make sure the flow is: generate token -> return plaintext to caller -> store hash in DB.

Two identical HasPermissionAttribute classes exist in different namespaces. Controllers import from Identity.Api.Authorization (e.g., RolesController.cs:2), while the Infrastructure one may be used elsewhere or unused.

title: [M6] Validate API key scopes against PermissionType enum
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
When creating an API key, the request.Scopes list is passed without validating that the scopes are legitimate permission values.

## Finding
**File:** src/Modules/Identity/Foundry.Identity.Api/Controllers/ApiKeysController.cs:85-91

Scopes are passed directly to the API key service without validating against known PermissionType enum values.

In ApiKeyAuthenticationMiddleware.cs:80-86, when scopes are present, they're added as raw 'scope' claims. The PermissionExpansionMiddleware then maps them via MapScopeToPermission(), which silently ignores unknown scopes.

Also related (from auth-investigator): API key auth middleware creates ClaimsIdentity with scopes but no role claims. PermissionExpansionMiddleware checks for 'azp' claim (service accounts) or role claims (users) — API key requests have neither, so permissions may not be properly expanded.

## Impact
Users can create API keys with arbitrary scope strings. Unknown scopes are silently ignored rather than rejected. API key permission expansion may be incomplete.

## Required Fix
1. Add validation in the CreateApiKey endpoint: reject scopes that don't map to valid PermissionType values
2. Verify PermissionExpansionMiddleware handles the auth_method=api_key case explicitly
3. Add tests for API key scope validation and permission expansion

title: [M8] Add FluentValidation validators for Configuration module commands
description:
## Severity: MEDIUM

## Summary
9 commands across the codebase lack FluentValidation validators. Most critically, all 5 Configuration/FeatureFlag commands have no input validation — no length limits, no format checks.

## Finding
Commands missing validators:

**Configuration module (highest priority — ALL commands lack validators):**
- CreateFeatureFlagCommand (Key, Name, Description, FlagType, DefaultEnabled, RolloutPercentage)
- UpdateFeatureFlagCommand (Id, Name, Description, DefaultEnabled, RolloutPercentage)
- DeleteFeatureFlagCommand (Id)
- CreateOverrideCommand (FlagId, TenantId, UserId, IsEnabled, Percentage)
- DeleteOverrideCommand (Id)

**Communications module (lower priority — ID-only commands):**
- MarkAllNotificationsReadCommand (UserId only)
- MarkNotificationReadCommand (NotificationId, UserId)

**Identity module (lower priority — ID-only commands):**
- RevokeServiceAccountCommand (Id only)
- RotateServiceAccountSecretCommand (Id only)

## Impact
For Configuration commands with multiple string/nullable parameters: no input length limits, no format validation. A user could submit arbitrarily long strings, invalid rollout percentages (negative, >100), etc.

## Required Fix
1. Create validators for all 5 Configuration commands:
   - CreateFeatureFlagValidator: validate key format (alphanumeric/dashes, max length), name length, description length, rollout percentage (0-100 range), valid FlagType enum
   - UpdateFeatureFlagValidator: same constraints, validate Id is not empty
   - DeleteFeatureFlagValidator: validate Id not empty
   - CreateOverrideValidator: validate FlagId not empty, percentage 0-100
   - DeleteOverrideValidator: validate Id not empty
2. Optionally add validators for the ID-only commands in Communications and Identity (lower priority since the IDs are validated by the repository lookup)
notes: AUDIT 2026-02-28: Only CustomFieldValidator exists. All 5 FeatureFlag command validators still missing. Communications/Identity ID-only commands also still lack validators.

title: [H4] Apply rate limiting policies to endpoints
description:
## Severity: HIGH

## Summary
Three rate limiting policies are registered (auth, upload, global) but zero endpoints use [EnableRateLimiting]. The UseRateLimiter() middleware is registered but does nothing. Authentication endpoints can be brute-forced without throttling.

## Finding
**File:** src/Foundry.Api/Extensions/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs:135-164

Policies defined:
- 'auth': 5 requests per 5 minutes (fixed window)
- 'upload': 10 requests per hour (fixed window)
- 'global': 1000 requests per hour (fixed window)

A codebase-wide search for [EnableRateLimiting], [DisableRateLimiting], or RequireRateLimiting returns ZERO results. No endpoint or controller applies any rate limiting policy.

**File:** src/Foundry.Api/Extensions/RateLimitDefaults.cs — Constants defined but unused.

## Impact
No rate limiting is enforced anywhere. Authentication endpoints can be brute-forced. Upload endpoints have no throttling. The global rate limit is purely decorative.

## Required Fix
1. Apply [EnableRateLimiting("auth")] to auth endpoints (AuthController)
2. Apply [EnableRateLimiting("upload")] to upload endpoints (StorageController upload actions)
3. Consider setting a global fallback limiter via options.GlobalLimiter for all other endpoints
4. Consider per-IP vs per-user rate limiting keys (currently using fixed window with no partition key, so it's global across all users). For auth, partition by IP. For upload, partition by user.
5. Add tests to verify rate limiting returns 429

title: [L6] Have Dapper services inject ITenantContext instead of accepting tenantId parameter
description:
## Severity: LOW

## Finding
**Files:** InvoiceQueryService.cs, InvoiceReportService.cs, PaymentReportService.cs, RevenueReportService.cs

These Dapper services take Guid tenantId as a method parameter rather than reading from ITenantContext. This means the calling code must pass the correct tenant ID — if a caller passes the wrong tenant ID, data leaks across tenants.

Related to M1 (centralized Dapper tenant filtering) but more specific.

## Fix
Change these services to inject ITenantContext and read TenantId from it, removing the tenantId method parameter. This makes tenant isolation automatic rather than caller-dependent.

title: Resolve Async suffix naming policy (1,221 IDE1006 warnings)
description:
1,221 IDE1006 warnings for 'Missing suffix: Async' on async methods. This is a POLICY DECISION — most of these are intentional naming patterns, not code defects.

**Root causes:**
1. Wolverine handlers use 'Handle' / 'HandleAsync' by convention — .editorconfig already relaxes this to 'suggestion' but dotnet format still flags them
2. ASP.NET controller actions don't conventionally use Async suffix
3. Some internal async methods genuinely missing Async suffix

**Options (pick one):**

**Option A (Recommended): Suppress in .editorconfig for known patterns**
- Set the async naming rule severity to 'none' in .editorconfig (it's currently 'suggestion')
- OR add more specific suppressions for controller/handler methods
- Rationale: The Wolverine and ASP.NET conventions are well-established. Fighting them creates noise.

**Option B: Add Async suffix everywhere**
- Rename all 1,221 methods to add Async suffix
- Would require updating all callers
- NOT recommended — breaks Wolverine conventions

**Option C: Selective enforcement**
- Keep 'suggestion' severity (current setting)
- Don't add dotnet format check for IDE1006 to CI
- Only enforce via PR review
- This is effectively the current state

**Implementation:**
1. Decide which option to take
2. If Option A: Update .editorconfig to set async naming rule severity to 'none' (or 'silent')
3. Verify with 'dotnet format Foundry.sln --verify-no-changes' that IDE1006 Async warnings are gone
4. Note: The _ prefix IDE1006 warnings are handled by separate tasks

**Important:** This task has NO file overlap with the other code quality tasks — it only touches .editorconfig rules, not source files.

title: [L1] Fix case-sensitive role matching in RolePermissionMapping
description:
## Severity: LOW

## Finding
**File:** src/Modules/Identity/Foundry.Identity.Infrastructure/Authorization/RolePermissionMapping.cs:43-44

Role names are compared case-sensitively: .Where(r => _rolePermissions.ContainsKey(r)). Dictionary keys are lowercase ('admin', 'manager', 'user'). If Keycloak sends 'Admin' (uppercase), the mapping silently fails.

TenantResolutionMiddleware already handles this with StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase (line 78), but RolePermissionMapping does not.

## Fix
Use a case-insensitive dictionary (StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase) or normalize role names to lowercase before lookup.

title: Audit and fix N+1 query risks in repositories
description:
VERIFIED: Multiple repositories use .Include() which could mask N+1 risks depending on usage patterns.

Repositories with Include() calls (8 total):
- Onboarding/OnboardingRepository.cs (7 Include calls - StepProgress, Steps)
- Configuration/FeatureFlagRepository.cs (1 Include - Overrides)
- Assets/AssetRepository.cs (2 Include - Variants)
- StatusPage/IncidentRepository.cs (1 Include - Updates)
- Billing/InvoiceRepository.cs (1 Include - LineItems)
- Comments/CommentRepository.cs (4 Include - Mentions, Reactions)
- Announcements/ChangelogRepository.cs (5 Include - Items)

Task: Review each repository's query patterns for N+1 risks. Check that:
1. Include() is used on queries that actually need related data
2. No queries load collections and then iterate with additional DB calls
3. Bulk operations use proper batch queries
4. Consider AsSplitQuery() for queries with multiple collection Includes

Highest risk: InventoryService.GetBulkAvailabilityAsync, Catalog hierarchical relationships

title: Optimize FlushUsageJob Redis key scanning
description:
File: src/Modules/Billing/Foundry.Billing.Infrastructure/Jobs/FlushUsageJob.cs:45-50

Issue: Uses server.KeysAsync("meter:*") which collects all matching keys into an unbounded List<RedisKey>.

Fix: Use a Redis Set to track active meter keys instead of SCAN. Add meter keys to the set when incrementing, remove after flushing.

title: Gate auto-migration behind environment check
description:

title: Standardize domain event naming across EF Core modules
description:
VERIFIED: Mixed domain event naming suffixes across EF Core modules.

Current patterns:
- '{Entity}{Action}DomainEvent': Billing, Email, Notifications (most explicit)
- '{Entity}{Action}Event': Catalog, Assets, Identity (shorter)
- '{Action}' only: Sales, Inventory, Scheduling (event-sourced - acceptable by convention)

Target: Standardize EF Core modules on one suffix. Recommend '{Entity}{Action}DomainEvent' for clarity.
Event-sourced modules (Sales, Inventory, Scheduling) keep short names by Marten convention.

NOTE: Related to foundry-8cf (naming conventions). Link this task to foundry-8cf.
Also related to P3 integration event naming (#27) - integration events in Shared.Contracts should use 'Event' suffix consistently.
